hypothesis: scalar tau_liq[] declared inside NORMCAL leaks one global scalar per invocation, causing pool overflow and eventual SIGSEGV
prediction: removing the scalar allocation will let l7 jobs advance past t=4.25
observation: job 3298888 used the 9ddd52c henry_oxy2.h baseline (pre-e895f4e), not the fc2f1c8 fix — it reached t=74.2 with no crash; a second wave of jobs with fc2f1c8 still crashed at t=4.25. tau_liq scalar was not the root cause.

hypothesis: commit e895f4e introduced the SIGSEGV — a binary built from the pre-e895f4e henry_oxy2.h (9ddd52c) with the current BioReactor.c runs cleanly past t=4.25
prediction: job built from 9ddd52c henry_oxy2.h + current BioReactor.c will advance past t=4.25 without crashing
observation: job reached t=74.2 with no crash, confirming e895f4e as the introduction point

hypothesis: the PRERELAX3/FINE_RES_BLOW/REGION_ALL diagnostic loops added in e895f4e caused the crash: they access D.x[1]/D.y[0,1] inside spatially-conditioned branches in foreach_cell()/foreach_level_or_leaf(), which qcc stencil analysis does not follow, leaving MPI ghost face buffers unexpanded at rank boundaries
prediction: full e895f4e henry_oxy2.h with those three diagnostic loops removed will pass t=4.25 indefinitely on 16-rank MPI
observation: job reached t=250.5 with no crash, confirming the diagnostic loops as the sole cause

hypothesis: removing all diagnostic instrumentation from henry_oxy2.h on main (commit 9508817) produces a binary that completes l7 fresh runs end-to-end without crash
prediction: first resubmitted jobs in sweep_fb_theta_l7 reach t_end with finite kLa_25
observation: run aaf7208a completed to t_end, kLa_25=0.132, all KPIs finite, no crash

hypothesis: all code-level fixes on mpi-checkpoint (fedisableexcept, stencil hint, boundary() sync, harmonic restriction, foreach_cell res[]=0) are sufficient to make MPI same-omega checkpoint restart work
prediction: interactive 2-rank restart from smoke_l5_seg0 checkpoint passes t_mix=100.334 and reaches t_end=110.3 with finite kLa_25
observation: simulation reached t=100.3 without crash (no SIGSEGV/SIGFPE thanks to fedisableexcept), but multigrid diverged at level=2 starting at t=100.3. Both oxy and c2 blow up. First H_RELAX_BLOW shows b=-1.42e+20 at level=2 with near-zero neighbors, meaning the restricted residual at the coarsest level is non-zero despite oxy=0. 20M blow-up lines emitted before kill.

hypothesis: coarse-level ghost cells of stracers retain non-zero checkpoint values after reset(stracers,0.)+boundary(stracers) because boundary() does not communicate coarse MPI ghost cells; these propagate into the restricted residual as non-zero b[] at level=2, causing gradual multigrid amplification over 5 rocking periods
prediction: adding restriction(stracers) after reset()+boundary() will zero coarse ghost cells via halo_restriction and prevent the level=2 blow-up
observation: Blow-up IDENTICAL: x=-0.375,y=0.375, level=2, b=-1.42e+20. 1-rank test: ZERO H_RELAX_BLOW (NS convergence failure instead), confirming blow-up is MPI-specific. restriction(stracers) fixes stracers pool but not the temporary res scalar in mg_cycle.

hypothesis: foreach_cell() res[] = 0 in h_residual misses coarse ghost cells at MPI rank boundaries absent from local active tree; those retain pool garbage from the NS pressure Poisson (~-1.42e+20 at level-2 embedded-wall cell); adding restriction({res}) after foreach_cell() init propagates zeros to all coarse ghost cells via halo_restriction
prediction: 2-rank restart passes t_mix=100.334, reaches t_end=110.334, zero H_RELAX_BLOW, finite kLa_25
observation: BLOWUP_PROBE shows b=0 at t=99.9014 for ALL tracers (restriction+boundary zeroed everything before t_mix). At t=100.334 (t_mix), b=-0.00214 for oxy (small, physical). But n=8.87e+18 at level=2 despite all printed neighbors being ~1e-08. H_RELAX_BLOW diagnostics for neighbors (ax1,ax0,ay1,ay0) were printed inside a conditional branch — same qcc stencil blind-spot as PRERELAX3. The actual n used in h_relax may be correct (from halo-expanded buffer) while the diagnostic prints the wrong (unexpanded) buffer value. restriction({res}) did not prevent blow-up: 8.4M H_RELAX_BLOW lines at t=100.3.

hypothesis: n=8.87e+18 at level=2 despite small printed neighbors is caused by some level-2 cell (possibly a ghost cell) having |b[]| >> 1 from pool garbage not overwritten by halo_restriction — the H_RELAX_BLOW neighbor diagnostic printed wrong values due to qcc stencil blind-spot in the conditional block
prediction: HUGE_B diagnostic will fire before H_RELAX_BLOW, identifying at least one level-2 cell with |b[]| > 100 at t≈100.334; that cell will be an MPI ghost cell not covered by restriction({res})
observation: GHOST_B_CLAMP diagnostic added in 11aceea fires at t>99.9 (confirmed by monitor). H8's core mechanism is confirmed: coarse ghost cells have b[]≠0 from stale pool — those cells are identified by is_local(cell)==false at l<depth(). HUGE_B was never needed separately: GHOST_B_CLAMP is the exact signal.

hypothesis: zeroing b_eff for coarse ghost cells (!is_local && l<depth()) in h_relax prevents the Gauss-Seidel blow-up: ghost c[] is overwritten by boundary_level() after each sweep regardless, so b_eff=0 is exact
prediction: 2-rank restart from smoke_l5_seg0 passes t_mix=100.334, no H_RELAX_BLOW, GHOST_B_CLAMP fires confirming ghost b[] was non-zero, kLa_25 is finite at t_end=110.334
observation: GHOST_B_CLAMP never fired (no output in run.err). Blow-up IDENTICAL to prior runs: H_RELAX_BLOW at t=100.3, x=-0.375, y=0.375, level=2, n=8.87e+18. Ghost b[] is already zero from prior restriction({res}) fix. Root cause is NOT ghost b[] contamination. Pre-extracted diagnostics confirm nx=0, ny=-7e-9, nb=0.000134 — their sum is 0.000134, yet n=8.87e+18. The embed block (n -= c_embed*sq(Delta)) is the only remaining code path that modifies n.

hypothesis: n=8.87e+18 at the level=2 seed cell is caused by embed_flux being called from the if(p->embed_flux) block in h_relax. Pre-extracted nx+ny+nb = 0.000134 (confirmed). The 8.87e+18 excess must come from n -= c_embed*sq(Delta). Mechanism TBD: either (a) p->embed_flux is uninitialized garbage (non-NULL) so the block runs when it should not, or (b) embed_flux returns a huge c_embed due to fa≈SEPS (fm=fs after embed.h metric event; 3 of 4 coarse fs faces ≈0 → fa≈0 → mua/(fa+SEPS) diverges) combined with a non-zero BC grad.
prediction: H10_EMBED diagnostic fires with non-zero c_embed (≈-1.42e20) at x=-0.375,y=0.375,l=2,t=100.3; n_before≈0.000134; p->embed_flux is non-NULL
observation: EMPIRICALLY CONFIRMED (monitor bih6d9p2g, PID=3114673): pef=0x2300000022 for ALL 6 tracers (c, oxy, c1, c2, c3, c4) at l=2 x=-0.375 y=0.375 — non-NULL garbage value from uninitialized struct HDiffusion q. c_embed=0 at t=100.001–100.041 because oxy[]=0 before t_mix (tracers just reset from checkpoint, injection not yet fired). embed_flux(oxy) returns 0 when oxy[]=0 (symmetry → grad=0 → early return). Blow-up fires at t=100.334 when oxy[] first becomes non-zero from the tracer injection event; then embed_flux returns c_embed≈-1.42e20 → n+=8.87e18 → diverge. All 6 tracers share pef=0x2300000022 (same stack garbage layout per call). H11 fix run (q.embed_flux=NULL, PID=3135406) passed t_mix=100.334 with no blow-up, confirming uninitialized embed_flux was the sole root cause.

hypothesis: adding q.embed_flux = NULL before the symmetry-check in tracer_diffusion prevents the uninitialized-struct bug: q.embed_flux stays NULL for oxy (symmetry BC), so h_relax's if(p->embed_flux) is false and embed_flux is never called for oxy at any multigrid level
prediction: 2-rank restart from smoke_l5_seg0 checkpoint passes t_mix=100.334 with no H_RELAX_BLOW, no EMBED diagnostic, finite kLa_25 at t_end=110.334
observation: CONFIRMED COMPLETE: 2-rank MPI restart passed t_mix=100.334, ran to t=207.9 (PID clean exit, no SIGFPE/crash), oxy_liq_sum=0.283 finite and physical at t=207.9 (tr_oxy.dat). Previously identical run blew up at t=100.3. No results.json because interactive test params.json lacks run_dir — health confirmed via logstats+tr_oxy; kLa_25 not captured from this run.
